item.title: Trump’s green new deal
item.description: The US president’s actions have inadvertently raised the appeal of renewable energy

item.title: US blocking tactics delay global shipping carbon levy talks
item.description: Legal adoption of deal agreed by majority a year ago pushed into limbo

item.title: Francisco Partners in talks to buy payments company Moneris
item.description: Royal Bank of Canada and Bank of Montreal are latest North American banks trying to offload a payments processor

item.title: The silent treatment: Saudi Arabia’s long game for managing Opec
item.description: Middle East conflict forces Riyadh to postpone any response to the UAE’s decision to quit the oil cartel

item.title: Detroit carmakers warn of $5bn commodities shock due to Iran war
item.description: Sector faces rising prices for supplies from aluminium to plastics and paint

item.title: Chicken wars ruffle feathers in Paris suburb
item.description: Leftist politicians trade barbs over Master Poulet chain

item.title: Inside the Swedish factory helping to re-arm Europe
item.description: BAE’s Hägglunds is increasing capacity as it gears up for a huge joint order from several of the region’s armies

item.title: IMF criticises EU governments for ignoring energy subsidy warnings
item.description: Fund urges countries to target support at the most vulnerable rather than using expensive blanket measures

item.title: Everyone loves Nintendo — except investors
item.description: Higher memory chip costs fuel fears of price rise for Switch 2 and cast shadow over console’s success

item.title: Investors pile into clean energy as Iran war drives push for energy security
item.description: Renewables funds attract biggest flows for five years, as focus shifts from climate change to geopolitical crisis

item.title: Why co-CEOs might suit our tumultuous times
item.description: Sharing the leadership burden remains rare but is not without benefits

item.title: Vinted challenges the threadbare finances of second-hand fashion
item.description: The company is now worth more than twice as much as it was in 2021

item.title: Start-ups challenge Apple over curbs on AI ‘vibe coding’ apps
item.description: iPhone maker warns about security risks as new software floods its review process

item.title: Private equity investor body sounds alarm on ‘conflict vehicles’
item.description: Continuation vehicles and rush for retail money are driving problematic behaviour by industry, warns ILPA executive

item.title: Software group IFS gears up for listing amid AI unease
item.description: CEO Mark Moffat says industrial software group will consider London, Europe and New York for IPO

item.title: Canada exchange group aims to spur mining listings boom in Australia
item.description: TMX has bought ASX rival and wants to attract companies that struggle to list on country’s primary exchange

item.title: Greg Abel’s message to Berkshire shareholders, post-Buffett: be patient
item.description: New CEO ‘not anxious to deploy capital into subpar opportunities’ as he picks up baton from legendary predecessor

item.title: Trump administration fast-tracks $8.6bn in arms sales to Middle East allies
item.description: Move to shore up regional partners during Iran war comes as US warns Europe of delays to weapons shipments

item.title: Pentagon to pull 5,000 soldiers out of Germany in dispute over Iran war
item.description: Donald Trump retaliates against Friedrich Merz over chancellor’s criticism of US-Israeli war effort

item.title: Berkshire cash pile climbs to $380bn as stock sales streak hits 14th quarter
item.description: Greg Abel to address shareholders at first gathering since he succeeded Warren Buffett

item.title: Canada’s diamond industry begins a long goodbye
item.description: The closure of Rio Tinto’s Diavik mine is the beginning of the end for a lifeline of the remote Northwest Territories

item.title: US warns Europe of delays to arms shipments as Iran war drains stockpiles
item.description: Deferred deliveries could include those used in Ukraine’s defence against Russia

item.title: Adidas sprints ahead of Nike in recovery race
item.description: Also in today’s newsletter: LIV’s dim prospects and the NBA’s proposed ‘tanking’ fix

item.title: Spirit Airlines winds down operations after bailout talks founder
item.description: Trump said US would only proceed with rescue for bankrupt budget carrier ‘if it’s a good deal’

item.title: The physical world strikes back
item.description: The Iran war is a reminder that geographic facts rather than digital tech shape our lives

item.title: The problem with healthy life expectancy
item.description: A crude blend of very different statistics is not the best tool for the job

item.title: Meta stock might look cheap if it weren’t for Mark Zuckerberg
item.description: Not only is the Facebook boss spinning a lot of plates, but his worldview is not like that of a typical chief executive

item.title: Wall Street traders post triple gains of European rivals
item.description: Europe’s biggest investment banks missed out on gains from commodities in quarter marked by oil swings

item.title: Dividend payments from UK companies up ‘over a fifth’ on last year
item.description: Total payouts were £16.4bn during the first quarter of 2026

item.title: The luxuries I can’t give up (even as prices rise)
item.description: I know it’s ridiculous to pay £32 for shower gel, but not all luxuries are frivolous
